summary: ISS-1590 (minor, EXP-256 c1 H6: "Condition 3 shows 0 / 69 but eg-falsifiability source does not explicitly state the denominator as 69; should use build-time interpolation (counts.total) rather than hardcoded 69") is already-fixed-by-source. The recommended fix (use ${counts.total} interpolation) was already in the original EXP-256 proposal — verbatim grep of monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-256-overview-falsification-module.json patch_segments[0].insert_text shows 0 / ${counts.total} in all four condition status spans from the outset. Current build-scripts/generate-html.js @ commit 4ee7c952 lines 1518/1526/1531 preserve the interpolation (verified by grep — zero hardcoded "0 / 69" in source; the rendered "0 / 69" in docs/index.html is the runtime evaluation of counts.total === 69, the dome’s claimed win count). EXP-271 remediation (commit ac474a95 "Operator rescue: ship EXP-271 falsifiability-module integration") preserved the interpolation while migrating to ds- prefix and tightening Conditions 3+4 framing to "0 / N met" (per ISS-1656 follow-up, also closed via consolidation). test.js lines 712-746 (EXP-271 test block) test-lock the rendering: assertEq(zeroNumCount, 2, "exactly 2 '0 / N met' spans (conditions 3, 4)"). The c1 H6 reviewer appears to have misread the proposal as having a hardcoded literal 69; in fact the proposal used interpolation from the outset. Adjacent concerns are tracked separately: ISS-1653 (denominator mismatch with verdict bar) consolidated into baby-EXP-386; ISS-1656 (Condition 1 attempt-vs-fail conflation) consolidated into baby-EXP-385. ISS-1590 closes cleanly as already-fixed-by-source with no patch, no test, no rebuild required. Verified verbatim by analyst-opus 2026-05-16 (clone @ commit 4ee7c952).
